system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

);

blocks
(
    hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5)        (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)   // Block 0
    hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11)      (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)   // Block 1
    hex (4 7 11 5 12 15 10 8)    (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)   // Block 2

);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {

            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (16 17 18 19)
        );

);

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_C_20260516_195157_run2/runs/squareBend/3/system/blockMeshDict/vertices at line 15.

    From function Foam::Istream& Foam::operator>>(Istream&, doubleScalar&)
    in file lnInclude/Scalar.C at line 101.

FOAM exiting"}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Token parsing error

**Error Message:**
```
wrong token type - expected Scalar, found on line 15 the punctuation token ','
```

**Diagnosis:**

The error occurs at line 15 of `blockMeshDict`, which corresponds to the first vertex entry:
```
(-50,  25,  25)   // 0
```

The issue is subtle: the OpenFOAM ASCII format expects **no spaces between the closing parenthesis and the next token**. When there's a space before the comment (`//`), the parser encounters the comma followed by whitespace and then the comment marker, causing the tokenization error.

Specifically, the format should be strictly:
```
(-50, 25, 25)// 0
```

Not:
```
(-50,  25,  25)   // 0
```

The extra spaces around the comma and before `//` confuse the CSV-style reader.

**Resolution:**

Remove all extra spaces in the `vertices` section. Every vertex entry must have **no space between the closing parenthesis and the comment marker**:

```
vertices
(
    (-50,25,25)// 0
    (-50,25,-25)// 1
    (-50,-25,-25)// 2
    (-50,-25,25)// 3

    (0,25,25)// 4
    (0,25,-25)// 5
    (0,-25,-25)// 6
    (0,-25,25)// 7

    (25,-25,25)// 8
    (25,-25,-25)// 9
    (25,0,25)// 10
    (25,0,-25)// 11

    (25,25,25)// 12
    (25,25,-25)// 13
    (25,-75,25)// 14
    (25,-75,-25)// 15

    (-500,-75,25)// 16
    (-500,-75,-25)// 17
);

```

All vertex coordinate tuples should have commas with exactly one space after them (which is fine), but crucially **no space between the closing parenthesis and the comment marker**.

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cannot find "points"

**Diagnosis:**

This is a cascading error. Since `blockMesh` failed, no mesh exists. This resolves automatically once `blockMesh` succeeds.

## Summary

The sole fix is in **`system/blockMeshDict`**: remove all extra spaces in the `vertices` section so that each vertex entry is formatted as `(-x,y,z)// index` with no space between `)` and `//`. Everything else remains unchanged.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'In the vertices section, remove all extra spaces between the closing parenthesis and the comment marker for each vertex entry; Change (-50,  25,  25)   // 0 to (-50,25,25)// 0; Change (-50,  25, -25)   // 1 to (-50,25,-25)// 1; Change (-50, -25, -25)   // 2 to (-50,-25,-25)// 2; Change (-50, -25,  25)   // 3 to (-50,-25,25)// 3; Change (  0,  25,  25)   // 4 to (0,25,25)// 4; Change (  0,  25, -25)   // 5 to (0,25,-25)// 5; Change (  0, -25, -25)   // 6 to (0,-25,-25)// 6; Change (  0, -25,  25)   // 7 to (0,-25,25)// 7; Change ( 25, -25,  25)   // 8 to (25,-25,25)// 8; Change ( 25, -25, -25)   // 9 to (25,-25,-25)// 9; Change ( 25,   0,  25)   // 10 to (25,0,25)// 10; Change ( 25,   0, -25)   // 11 to (25,0,-25)// 11; Change ( 25,  25,  25)   // 12 to (25,25,25)// 12; Change ( 25,  25, -25)   // 13 to (25,25,-25)// 13; Change ( 25, -75,  25)   // 14 to (25,-75,25)// 14; Change ( 25, -75, -25)   // 15 to (25,-75,-25)// 15; Change (-500, -75,  25)   // 16 to (-500,-75,25)// 16; Change (-500, -75, -25)   // 17 to (-500,-75,-25)// 17'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
